summary: High‑energy Virginia show with soaring China>Rider and smoking Space>Aiko
blurb: The second set delivers a blistering China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue, a fiery Space → Aiko jam, and tight performances of High Time and Estimated Prophet. Jerry’s guitar is hot throughout while Brent’s keyboards drive the transitions. Overall the show stays on a high‑energy groove from start to finish.
review: On September 14, 1982 at the University of Virginia the Dead turned in one of their most vibrant late‑summer performances. The first set opened with a rollicking Jack Straw and built momentum through Lazy Lightning into an inspired Supplication that set the tone for a night of high‑octane improvisation. The second set is where the magic really unfolded: China Cat Sunflower melted seamlessly into I Know You Rider, followed by a thunderous Space → Aiko excursion that had the crowd riding the wave of Jerry’s “sweet and hot” lead tones. High Time roared forward with tight rhythm from Bob and a booming drum foundation courtesy of Mickey and Billy, while the Estimated Prophet → He's Gone transition kept the energy rolling into a classic post‑drum sequence of Iko Iko, Truckin', Black Peter, and a jubilant Bertha to close. Across all six sources the performance is praised as tight, enthusiastic, and full of standout moments, making it a must‑listen snapshot of the 1982 era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings label the show as positive and two are mixed, but all highlight high‑energy playing, standout jams (China>Rider, Space>Aiko) and strong band cohesion. Consistent praise across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Bright, crystal‑clear soundboard that captures the full second set with excellent drum presence and no dropouts, delivering the most faithful representation of the show’s high‑energy flow.

key_highlights: Soaring China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Tight, high‑octane performances of High Time and Estimated Prophet
Exceptional audience capture of Truckin' and Supplication

band_performance.Jerry: High‑energy guitar work throughout, sweet yet hot tone on solos and leads, especially during Space → Aiko and High Time.
band_performance.Phil: Louder-than‑usual bass that cuts through the mix, solid foundation across both sets, standout moments on Deal.
band_performance.Bob: Dialed‑in rhythm and strong vocals, notable contributions in the Space → Aiko jam and classic C.C. Rider delivery.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work shines during China Cat Sunflower → Rider transition and drives the harmonic flow of the second set jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums bright and exceptionally well‑played, providing a powerful groove that propels the high‑energy moments.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming complementing Mickey, tight together to keep the rhythmic pulse steady throughout.
